hypothesis: In the MADE benchmark, a multi-fidelity, uncertainty-calibrated acquisition policy that screens with an MLIP and promotes to DFT only candidates with high epistemic uncertainty and predicted ∆hull ≤ 0.05 eV/atom will yield at least 40% more DFT-verified novel stable ternary compounds than a baseline that promotes the 30 lowest-MLIP-energy candidates under a fixed DFT budget of 30 evaluations.
